page.content: The report examines two tables detailing medical criteria and survival statistics. Table 1 presents classifications including "Multiple sclerosis" and "Clinically isolated syndrome," with headers for McDonald Criteria under IgM, IgG, and IgA, alongside metrics for pleocytosis (≥5 cells/μL), intrathecal synthesis, and CSF oligoclonal bands. For "Multiple sclerosis," the 2005/2001 criteria show values of 2005/2001, 70, 66%, 37%, 64%, 16%, and 98.5%. In 2010, the same classification shows values of 136, 65%, 35%, 63%, 13%, and 96%.

page.content: The p values for this classification are listed as p = 0.0001, p = 1.0, p = 0.9, p = 1.0, p = 0.7, and p = 0.4. For "Clinically isolated syndrome," the 2005/2001 metrics are 255, 55%, 19%, 40%, 6%, and 65%, while in 2010, the values are 189, 52%, 14%, 33%, 4%, and 55%, with p values of p = 0.0001, p = 0.8, p = 0.4, p = 0.4, p = 0.7, and p = 0.2. Table 2 focuses on survival metrics, with "ABCA1 methylation" yielding HR (95% CI) of 1.106 (1.008–1.213), P = 0.033a for overall survival and 1.096 (1.020–1.179), P = 0.013 for progression-free survival. Age metrics yield HR of 2.014 (0.712–5.701), P = 0.187 and 1.309 (0.579–2.963), P = 0.518. Stage shows HR of 11.321 (2.48–51.679), P = 0.02 and 14.278 (4.233–48.154), P < 0.01. Grade metrics indicate HR of 22.559 (2.849–178.624), P = 0.03 and 20.586 (4.817–87.977), P < 0.01.
